content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 15, 2018

Donna Mooney
Chief Assistant City Attorney
City Attorney's Office
555 Santa Clara Street
Vallejo, CA 94590

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-053

Dear Ms. Mooney:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Councilmember Robert
McConnell regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and
Gov. Code Section 1090.¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of
interest. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Solano County
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that,
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice is not admissible in a criminal proceeding
against any individual other than the requestor. (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Does the Act or Section 1090 prohibit City Councilmember McConnell, as a member of the
Vallejo Housing Authority ("VHA") Board of Commissioners, from entering into a housing
assistance payment contract with VHA so that he may accept a Section 8 Housing Choice voucher
from an existing market-rate tenant?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-053
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

Yes, the requirements for a remote interest under Section 1091(b)(12) are not met and the
contract is prohibited under Section 1090 as to the unit in question. No further analysis under the
Act is necessary where the contract is prohibited under Section 1090.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

Councilmember McConnell was elected to the City Council of the City of Vallejo in 2012.
As a city council member, he concurrently became a member of the Board of Commissioners of the
City of Vallejo's Housing Authority ("VHA").

The Vallejo Housing Authority offers a Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher ("HCV")
Program for state-approved private rental residency. The HCV Program offers housing subsidies to
eligible low-income individuals and families to rent existing, privately-owned housing units from
participating landlords. Payments are made through the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban
Development ("HUD") program to the property owner, who is under contract with the
VHA. These contracts are reviewed and executed by a VHA staff member who considers nine
factors established by the HUD including a determination that the rent amount is reasonable. You
state that the VHA Board of Commissioners does not play a role in individual housing assistance
payment contracts. The Board considers and vote on policies, programs operations and strategies to
meet local housing needs and goals.

Councilmember McConnell owns twenty-eight rental units in the City, of which five are
currently Section 8 units. Prior to his election in 2012, he had entered into several HCV Section 8
contracts with VHA to receive Section 8 vouchers from tenants. Upon his election, the City
Attorney determined that he had a remote interest in these contracts under Section 1091(b)(12), as
the contracts were executed prior to his election to the city council and becoming a VHA
commissioner. The City Attorney also determined that Councilmember McConnell was in
compliance with the Act, so long as he disclosed his interest and refrained from participating in the
renewal or extension of the contracts. Because of this determination, the HUD field office found
good cause to waive the federal regulation prohibiting any public official or member of a governing
body from having an interest in a housing assistance payment program under 24 C.F.R. Section
982.161.

An existing market-rate tenant of Councilmember McConnell's (the "Tenant") was recently
issued a Section 8 voucher. She lives in one of three units on a parcel. At the time that
Councilmember McConnell purchased the property, the unit at issue was subject to a housing
assistance payment contract with a different tenant. However, this contract terminated when the
original tenant vacated the unit several years ago. One other unit on the parcel is subject to a
housing assistance payment contract executed prior to Councilmember McConnell's election and
continued since that time. The Tenant's Section 8 voucher expires in June 2018 unless extended.
The Tenant wishes to stay in her present residence. She has searched for places to rent that accept
her Section 8 voucher, but she has not located a unit.

You indicate that the City of Vallejo does not keep statistics on rental housing vacancies.
However, you have researched this issue and provide information from the Vallejo Economic

File No. A-18-053
Page No. 3

Trends Report, prepared for the City in 2015, that the rental housing vacancy rate fell from 5.6% in 2006 to a rate of 3.2% in 2014. Also, the you state that the U.S. Census Bureau Rental Vacancy Rates for fourth quarter 2017 for the three areas closest in proximity to the City are: 4.5 percent for Sacramento-Roseville-Arden-Arcade, 4.8 percent for San Francisco-Oakland-Hayward, and 1.4 percent for San Jose-Sunnyvale-Santa Clara.

Additionally, you have stated that federal regulations prohibit the VHA from entering into any contract or arrangement in connection with the HCV program with any public official or member of a governing body who exercises functions or responsibilities with respect to the programs, where that official has any interest, direct or indirect. This conflict of interest prohibition may be waived by the HUD filed office for good cause, as noted above.

ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 1090

Section 1090 provides that public officers, including elected city officials, shall not be financially interested in any contract made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are members. Under Section 1090 the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has a financial interest.

Board Members Presumed Participation

Board members are conclusively presumed to have made any contract executed by the board under its jurisdiction, even if the board member has disqualified himself or herself from any and all participation in the making of the contract. Therefore, if a board member is financially interested in the contract, and no exception applies, Section 1090 prohibits the contract from being made. Although you state that the VHA Board does not play a role in individual housing assistance payment contracts, which are reviewed and executed by VHA staff; the program, its policies and operations are under the jurisdiction and control of the VHA Board.

Remote and Noninterest Exceptions

The Legislature has expressly defined certain financial interests as "remote" or "noninterest" exceptions to Section 1090's general prohibition. Where a remote interest is present, the contract may be lawfully executed provided (1) the officer discloses his or her financial interest in the contract to the public agency; (2) the interest is noted in the public body's official records; and (3) the officer completely abstains from any participation in the making of the contract. (Section 1091.) Where a noninterest is present, the contract may be executed without the abstention. (Section 1091.5.)

As pertinent to your question, Section 1091(b)(12) addresses the financial interest of an elected officer in a Section 8 housing assistance payment contracts where the officer is the landlord. Under this section, an officer has a "remote interest" if the interest is:

File No. A-18-053
Page No. 4

"(12) That of an elected officer otherwise subject to Section 1090, in any
housing assistance payment contract entered into pursuant to Section 8 of the United
States Housing Act of 1937 (42 U.S.C. Sec. 1437f) as amended, provided that the
housing assistance payment contract was in existence before Section 1090 became
applicable to the officer and will be renewed or extended only as to the existing
tenant, or, in a jurisdiction in which the rental vacancy rate is less than 5 percent, as
to new tenants in a unit previously under a Section 8 contract. This section applies to
any person who became a public official on or after November 1, 1986."

This matter does not involve an existing housing assistance payment contract, either as to an
existing tenant or this unit. Councilmember McConnell's interest does not meet the requirements of
Section 1091(b)(12) regarding the Tenant's present unit. When an official's financial interest is
generally described by a statutory exception and fails to satisfy all the conditions attached to that
exception, the exception is deemed inapplicable, and the general prohibition under Section 1090
applies. (98 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 102, 108 (2015).) Therefore, Councilmember McConnell and the
VHA Board are prohibited from entering into such a contract under Section 1090. We note that the
remote exception would apply to the Tenant only as a new tenant in one of Mr. McConnell's units
with an existing Section 8 contract, and only so long as the vacancy rate is less than five percent in
the City.

Because Councilmember McConnell is prohibited under Section 1090 from entering into a
contract, no further analysis is required under the Act. Please note that where an existing housing
assistance payment contract does meet the requirements of a remote interest, Councilmember
McConnell must continue to disclose his interest to the VHA Board, and recuse himself from any
participation in decisions related to his remote interest, as previously advised by the City Attorney.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Acting General Counsel

By: L. Karen Harrison
Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl

Government Code Section 1090 
Section I 090 provides that public officers, including elected city officials, shall not be 
financially interested in any contract made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or 
board of which they are members. Under Section 1090 the prohibited act is the making of a contract 
in which the official has a financial interest.

"(12) That of an elected officer otherwise subject to Section 1090, in any
